prompt: A manufacturing company produces widgets. Each widget requires 2.5 kg of raw material, costing $8 per kg. Labor and overhead costs are $15 per *successfully manufactured* widget. In any production run, 10% of the *total items produced* are found to be defective and cannot be sold; these defective items still incur raw material costs but no labor or overhead. If a batch of 200 items is produced, what should be the selling price per *good* widget if the company aims for a 25% profit margin on the *total cost incurred to produce the good items*?
